sub_instance.problem_statement:
Coastal Emergency scenario:
Storm season is coming. You need to wire a resilient emergency communication network across coastal villages.

You may choose any subset of the available cable links, but the chosen cable network must be a single connected tree that includes every required terminal village.
Every village that lies in your chosen network is active and must be assigned a radio frequency.
If an interference pair is active on both ends, those two villages must use different frequencies.

Objective:
total_cost = sum(cable costs used) + 15 * num_frequencies_used

Villages:
- Port, Bay, Cliff, Pine, Rock, Dune, Mill, Cape

Required terminals:
- Port, Rock, Mill

Available cable links:
- Bay-Port: cost 19
- Bay-Cliff: cost 5
- Bay-Pine: cost 17
- Bay-Rock: cost 12
- Bay-Dune: cost 7
- Bay-Mill: cost 2
- Bay-Cape: cost 3
- Cliff-Dune: cost 11
- Cliff-Mill: cost 4
- Pine-Port: cost 12
- Pine-Rock: cost 14
- Dune-Port: cost 18
- Dune-Pine: cost 11
- Dune-Rock: cost 6
- Dune-Mill: cost 6
- Mill-Port: cost 21
- Mill-Rock: cost 10
- Cape-Cliff: cost 2
- Cape-Rock: cost 12
- Cape-Dune: cost 9
- Cape-Mill: cost 2

Interference pairs:
- Port / Rock
- Bay / Mill
- Bay / Cape
- Pine / Port
- Dune / Port
- Dune / Pine
- Dune / Rock
- Cape / Mill

Rules:
- Your cable choice must be a tree, not a cyclic network.
- All required terminals must lie in one connected component.
- Frequencies matter only for active villages in the chosen network.
- If you stop immediately or fail to produce a valid answer, the baseline below is what gets scored.

Baseline total cost: 90
Baseline answer summary:
Edges: Dune-Port(18), Dune-Rock(6), Mill-Port(21)
Active villages: Port, Rock, Dune, Mill
Frequencies: Port=1, Rock=2, Dune=3, Mill=1
Edge cost: 45
Frequencies used: 3
Total cost: 90

Answer contract hint: Object with "edges": [["Port", "Bay"], ...] and "frequencies": {"Port": 1, ...}.

sub_instance.problem_statement:
You run a two-factory manufacturing pipeline. Factory A makes components, then Factory B finishes the same jobs.
Each factory is its own job shop with one-job-per-machine exclusivity and no preemption.
Every job must finish all of its Factory A operations before that same job may start any Factory B operation.
Your objective is to minimize the coupled makespan: the time when the final Factory B operation finishes.

Instance size: 5 jobs, 6 machines in each factory.
Jobs and ordered operations:
- Job 1: Factory A MA5 (9) -> MA3 (3) -> MA1 (2) -> MA4 (5) -> MA6 (6) -> MA2 (1); Factory B MB3 (3) -> MB1 (9) -> MB4 (2) -> MB2 (1) -> MB6 (6) -> MB5 (5)
- Job 2: Factory A MA6 (9) -> MA5 (3) -> MA1 (3) -> MA2 (7) -> MA4 (9) -> MA3 (7); Factory B MB3 (4) -> MB4 (4) -> MB6 (2) -> MB1 (2) -> MB5 (8) -> MB2 (5)
- Job 3: Factory A MA4 (8) -> MA1 (6) -> MA3 (3) -> MA5 (8) -> MA6 (5) -> MA2 (3); Factory B MB3 (4) -> MB4 (3) -> MB1 (5) -> MB6 (5) -> MB2 (2) -> MB5 (5)
- Job 4: Factory A MA5 (2) -> MA1 (1) -> MA3 (6) -> MA2 (7) -> MA6 (6) -> MA4 (3); Factory B MB3 (3) -> MB5 (7) -> MB6 (6) -> MB4 (8) -> MB2 (6) -> MB1 (5)
- Job 5: Factory A MA1 (5) -> MA5 (3) -> MA3 (8) -> MA4 (3) -> MA6 (6) -> MA2 (7); Factory B MB3 (3) -> MB1 (2) -> MB6 (8) -> MB5 (6) -> MB2 (3) -> MB4 (3)

Constraints:
- A machine handles at most one job at a time.
- Each job must respect its listed operation order inside each factory.
- Factory B job Jx cannot start until Factory A job Jx has fully completed.
- When you emit a schedule, every required operation must appear exactly once.

A deterministic feasible baseline schedule is provided below. If you stop immediately or fail to produce a valid schedule, this baseline is what gets scored.
Baseline makespan: 167
Baseline schedule summary:
Factory A: MA1: J1[12-14],J2[37-40],J3[64-70],J4[83-84],J5[84-89]; MA2: J1[25-26],J2[40-47],J3[86-89],J4[90-97],J5[115-122]; MA3: J1[9-12],J2[56-63],J3[70-73],J4[84-90],J5[92-100]; MA4: J1[14-19],J2[47-56],J3[56-64],J4[103-106],J5[106-109]; MA5: J1[0-9],J2[34-37],J3[73-81],J4[81-83],J5[89-92]; MA6: J1[19-25],J2[25-34],J3[81-86],J4[97-103],J5[109-115]
Factory B: MB1: J1[29-38],J2[73-75],J3[96-101],J4[140-145],J5[145-147]; MB2: J1[40-41],J2[83-88],J3[106-108],J4[134-140],J5[161-164]; MB3: J1[26-29],J2[63-67],J3[89-93],J4[106-109],J5[122-125]; MB4: J1[38-40],J2[67-71],J3[93-96],J4[126-134],J5[164-167]; MB5: J1[47-52],J2[75-83],J3[108-113],J4[113-120],J5[155-161]; MB6: J1[41-47],J2[71-73],J3[101-106],J4[120-126],J5[147-155]
Makespan: 167

Coastal Emergency scenario:
Storm season is coming. You need to wire a resilient emergency communication network across coastal villages.

You may choose any subset of the available cable links, but the chosen cable network must be a single connected tree that includes every required terminal village.
Every village that lies in your chosen network is active and must be assigned a radio frequency.
If an interference pair is active on both ends, those two villages must use different frequencies.

Objective:
total_cost = sum(cable costs used) + 15 * num_frequencies_used

sub_instance.problem_statement:
You run a two-factory manufacturing pipeline. Factory A makes components, then Factory B finishes the same jobs.
Each factory is its own job shop with one-job-per-machine exclusivity and no preemption.
Every job must finish all of its Factory A operations before that same job may start any Factory B operation.
Your objective is to minimize the coupled makespan: the time when the final Factory B operation finishes.

Instance size: 6 jobs, 7 machines in each factory.
Jobs and ordered operations:
- Job 1: Factory A MA5 (2) -> MA3 (5) -> MA7 (6) -> MA1 (1) -> MA4 (3) -> MA6 (9) -> MA2 (2); Factory B MB3 (1) -> MB1 (6) -> MB4 (5) -> MB7 (9) -> MB2 (1) -> MB6 (7) -> MB5 (3)
- Job 2: Factory A MA5 (9) -> MA6 (7) -> MA3 (4) -> MA7 (4) -> MA2 (2) -> MA4 (2) -> MA1 (8); Factory B MB2 (5) -> MB4 (7) -> MB5 (2) -> MB7 (5) -> MB3 (4) -> MB1 (3) -> MB6 (7)

- Job 4: Factory A MA7 (8) -> MA5 (6) -> MA3 (5) -> MA4 (1) -> MA1 (5) -> MA6 (9) -> MA2 (9); Factory B MB3 (1) -> MB4 (3) -> MB7 (5) -> MB2 (2) -> MB1 (9) -> MB6 (5) -> MB5 (2)
- Job 5: Factory A MA7 (6) -> MA6 (3) -> MA5 (3) -> MA4 (5) -> MA1 (8) -> MA2 (9) -> MA3 (9); Factory B MB4 (8) -> MB2 (1) -> MB3 (7) -> MB7 (8) -> MB1 (8) -> MB5 (7) -> MB6 (3)
- Job 6: Factory A MA2 (8) -> MA1 (5) -> MA4 (9) -> MA5 (2) -> MA3 (4) -> MA7 (3) -> MA6 (9); Factory B MB7 (5) -> MB5 (2) -> MB4 (5) -> MB6 (1) -> MB1 (2) -> MB2 (8) -> MB3 (8)

Constraints:

- Each job must respect its listed operation order inside each factory.
- Factory B job Jx cannot start until Factory A job Jx has fully completed.

Baseline schedule summary:

Factory B: MB1: J1[29-35],J2[76-79],J3[108-113],J4[120-129],J5[161-169],J6[184-186]; MB2: J1[49-50],J2[53-58],J3[90-97],J4[118-120],J5[145-146],J6[186-194]; MB3: J1[28-29],J2[72-76],J3[85-90],J4[109-110],J5[146-153],J6[194-202]; MB4: J1[35-40],J2[58-65],J3[76-85],J4[110-113],J5[137-145],J6[178-183]; MB5: J1[57-60],J2[65-67],J3[101-108],J4[134-136],J5[169-176],J6[176-178]; MB6: J1[50-57],J2[79-86],J3[97-101],J4[129-134],J5[176-179],J6[183-184]; MB7: J1[40-49],J2[67-72],J3[74-76],J4[113-118],J5[153-161],J6[168-173]
